messages: Parasites with this haplotype combination are considered "super resistant" to sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine. The prevalence of this haplotype is low in this geographical area, still the spread of these parasites can strongly influence the efficacy of sulfadoxine/pyrimethamine in this geographical area.

Parasites with this haplotype combination are considered "super resistant" to sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine. The prevalence of this haplotype is increased in this geographical area, therefore the efficacy of sulfadoxine/pyrimethamine is considered low in this geographical area.

Parasites with this haplotype combination are considered "super resistant" to sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine. The prevalence of this haplotype is very high in this geographical area, therefore the efficacy of sulfadoxine/pyrimethamine is considered low in this geographical area.

blurb: Full resistance to sulfadoxine/pyrimethamine.

guidelines: threshold based on own judgement, WHO states SP can still be effective in SMC and IPTp even if high prevalence of mutations

messages: Parasites with this haplotype combination are considered partial resistant to sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine. The prevalence of this haplotype is low in this geographical area.

Parasites with this haplotype combination are considered partial resistant to sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine. The prevalence of this haplotype is increased in this geographical area. SP used for seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) and IPTp in pregnancy could be ineffective.
